title: Embodiment Game
genericDescription: Use input, timing, constraint, and feedback to shape embodied experience; connect body and meaning; accommodate different bodies.
specificDescription: Build a game where physical interaction, rhythm, pace, posture, gesture, or sensory limitation matters. Include an alternative control or play mode.
gradingRubric: 30 pts embodied design; 20 pts conceptual connection; 20 pts accessibility; 15 pts polish; 15 pts reflection.
